items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
Shpak's tape recorder,
the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Matches the reference wording and preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения». However, the translation repeats the line twice (once as a continuation and then again as two separate subtitle lines), which is not motivated by the source and can feel like a subtitle duplication/glitch. Also, per reference analysis, the construction should avoid implying possession (“Shpak has… the ambassador has…”) because they lost the items; the current phrasing is elliptical and does not explicitly assert possession, so it is acceptable. Main problem is the duplicated rendering.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. The repetitive, mundane politeness is preserved and the era-clash humor works as intended.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the sudden authoritarian, pompous demand that drives the joke.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the archaic/high-register melancholy and the comic mismatch with the modern setting.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the subject-role of “demons” and the panicked, superstitious framing of a mundane technical situation.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the elevated religious exclamation and the joke of attributing a mechanical event to divine intervention.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; captures the MC/announcer vibe and the sudden mass-party command rather than a flat literal “Everyone dance!”.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the breezy self-satisfaction and opportunistic tone that makes the anti-hero charming.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the intended cultural joke and preventing confusion.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the comic inventory rhythm, the escalating prestige-listing, and the trailing self-correction feel.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the calm, sing-song resignation that turns a dark institutional threat into shared absurdity.

summary.interpretation: 0.18 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.04 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
